title: Anorectal Malformations: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Anorectal Malformations
Identify key diagnostic features of Anorectal Malformations
Apply clinical reasoning to Anorectal Malformations management
Recognize common pitfalls in Anorectal Malformations

sections.title: Overview of Anorectal Malformations
sections.content:
**Anorectal Malformations**



**Key Points:**
• Failure of urorectal septum to divide cloaca into urogenital sinus and anorectal canal
• Imperforate anus: spectrum from low (perineal fistula, good prognosis) to high (rectovesical fistula)
• High lesions: associated with other anomalies (VACTERL), require colostomy → definitive repair later

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Failure of urorectal septum to divide cloaca into urogenital sinus and anorectal canal
• Imperforate anus: spectrum from low (perineal fistula, good prognosis) to high (rectovesical fistula)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• High lesions: associated with other anomalies (VACTERL), require colostomy → definitive repair later
• Low lesions: perineal fistula visible, can do primary repair (anoplasty)
• Evaluate for VACTERL association: spine X-ray, renal US, echocardiogram, esophageal patency

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Anorectal Malformations:**

• High lesions: associated with other anomalies (VACTERL), require colostomy → definitive repair later
• Low lesions: perineal fistula visible, can do primary repair (anoplasty)
• Evaluate for VACTERL association: spine X-ray, renal US, echocardiogram, esophageal patency

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Anorectal Malformations?

options.text: Failure of urorectal septum to divide cloaca into urogenital sinus and anorectal canal
options.explanation: Failure of urorectal septum to divide cloaca into urogenital sinus and anorectal canal

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Anorectal Malformations?

options.text: Evaluate for VACTERL association: spine X-ray, renal US, echocardiogram, esophageal patency
options.explanation: Evaluate for VACTERL association: spine X-ray, renal US, echocardiogram, esophageal patency

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Anorectal Malformations. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Imperforate anus: spectrum from low (perineal fistula, good prognosis) to high (rectovesical fistula)
options.explanation: Imperforate anus: spectrum from low (perineal fistula, good prognosis) to high (rectovesical fistula)
